description: Lock the 2026-06-22 Renaissance source-check pass to source-backed dates and narrowed direct dependencies.

checks.reason: The Columbian Exchange node is anchored to Columbus's voyages beginning in 1492.
The exchange requires transoceanic exploration/contact as the enabling context.
Galleon ship design is not a source-supported direct prerequisite for the exchange's 1492 onset.
Botanical garden infrastructure supports later study, not the initial exchange.
The Slide Rule node is anchored to Oughtred's first adjustable logarithmic rule in 1632.
The Micrometers & Precision Measurement node is anchored to Gascoigne's early-1640s astronomical micrometer.
The source-backed micrometer path is telescope instrumentation, not slide-rule calculation.
Early instrument calibration practice predates the micrometer node and should not depend directly on it.
The Public Art Academies node is anchored to Florence's Accademia del Disegno in 1563.
The Printed Music Notation node is anchored to Petrucci's Harmonice Musices Odhecaton A in 1501.
Printed music by movable type belongs to printing-press lineage, not public art academy infrastructure.
